title: Threat Hunting in 2026
excerpt: How to find advanced threats early
content: Threat hunting works best when analysts combine good telemetry, a clear hypothesis, and disciplined investigation. Start with the most valuable assets, review authentication and endpoint anomalies, then correlate activity across identity, network, and cloud logs before escalating.
author: R. Singh

title: SOC Modernisation
excerpt: Building an efficient security operations centre
content: A modern SOC is less about volume and more about signal. Automation should remove repetitive triage work, while clear playbooks, metrics, and escalation paths help analysts focus on the highest-risk events.
author: L. Patel

title: Zero Trust Security Framework
excerpt: Implementing zero-trust architecture for maximum protection
content: Zero trust succeeds when identity, device health, and least privilege are enforced continuously. Every request should be evaluated with context instead of assuming trust because a user or device is already inside the network.
author: M. Chen

title: Building an Incident Response Playbook
excerpt: A practical framework for faster containment and recovery
content: A useful incident response playbook keeps roles, escalation triggers, and communication steps simple. Teams should be able to open it during pressure and immediately know what to isolate, who to notify, and how to preserve evidence.
author: A. Johnson

title: Cloud Security Posture Management
excerpt: How to keep cloud environments visible, compliant, and resilient
content: Cloud posture management works best as a continuous loop: discover assets, compare them against policy, prioritize drift, and close the highest-risk gaps first. Visibility is the foundation of every later control.
author: S. Khan

title: Phishing Awareness That Actually Works
excerpt: Designing training programs that reduce risky clicks
content: Awareness training changes behavior when it is practical, repeated, and tied to real examples. Simulated attacks, short feedback loops, and role-specific guidance produce better results than one-off presentations.
author: N. Walker

title: Reducing Supply Chain Risk
excerpt: Assessing third-party exposure before it becomes an incident
content: Third-party risk management should start with critical dependencies, not a long vendor checklist. Focus on the suppliers that touch sensitive data, identity, payments, or production systems and verify controls before issues spread.
author: K. Rivera

title: Security Automation for Lean Teams
excerpt: Where automation creates the biggest operational lift
content: Lean teams get the most value from automation when it handles alert enrichment, ticket creation, and repetitive containment tasks. The goal is to reduce manual toil so analysts can spend time making decisions, not copying data between tools.
author: D. Nguyen
